name: Robb's Special Cherry Pie
recipeIngredient: 2 (1 lb.) cans pitted tart red cherries (water pack)
recipeInstructions:
1. (DIRECTIONS FOR PASTRY)
Combine flour and salt in a large mixing bowl. Cut shortening into the flour mixture using a pastry blender until it resembles coarse crumbs with a few pea-sized pieces.
Sprinkle 4 tablespoons ice water over the mixture and toss with two forks.
Sprinkle another 4 tablespoons ice water over the mixture and toss again. If the mixture can be pressed together to form a solid mass, do not add additional water. If the mixture is still crumbly, add additional ice water tablespoon at a time, tossing the mixture with forks until a mass can be formed.
Divide the mixture into two balls, one slightly larger than the other. Press the larger ball of dough into a disk and roll between sheets of waxed paper until 1/8-inch thick. Line a deep 9-inch Pyrex pie plate with pastry and trim edges to 3/4 inch beyond the edge of the plate. Set aside.
Press the remaining ball of dough into a disk and roll between sheets of waxed paper until 1/8-inch thick. Set aside.
2. (DIRECTIONS FOR FILLING)
Drain cherries. Measure 1/3 cup liquid into a mixing bowl. Discard the remaining liquid.
Add tapioca, salt, almond extract, lemon juice, and food coloring to the cherry mixture.
Combine 1 cup sugar with cinnamon and cloves in another bowl. Pour this sugar mixture over the cherries.
Mix and let stand at least 15 minutes.
Fill pastry with cherry mixture. Dot with butter. Sprinkle with remaining sugar.
Moisten the rim with water. Fit top pastry over filling.
Turn the top edge over the bottom edge and press firmly to create fluted edges.
Cut vents in the top to allow steam to escape and juices to bubble.
Bake at 425 degrees for 15 minutes. Reduce temperature to 375 degrees and continue baking for 35-45 minutes, or until filling is bubbling and crust is golden brown. If the edge of the crust appears to be browning too quickly, cover lightly with aluminum foil.
Cool pie thoroughly on a wire rack.
Note: The spice flavor is quite subtle, but very effective when combined with the lemon juice and almond extract. Try these modest amounts first (I prefer them), or increase them to suit your taste.
